reasoning: The finding's subject (server-coverage.test.ts:849) is accurate at its abstraction level: for a terminated session validateSession throws McpError (server.ts:273-280) and handleEvaluateExpression's catch returns a normal non-throwing ServerResult with the error embedded in content (server.ts:1355-1360) — exactly why the test asserts on content text (:850) rather than expecting a throw. Not asserting payload.success is a test-thoroughness wish, a known FP mode. [Panel note: the sweep-era expected.json adjudicated the L1117 comment (also accurate vs server.ts:1529-1548); this acceptance re-anchors on the packaged finding.]
gray_reason: For the packaged finding (test :849 'returns a success response with the error in the content'), the payload literally carries success:false (server.ts:1360); a hyper-literal judge could argue confirm-at-info for misleading wording, though the MCP-level reading (returns rather than throws) is the natural one and matches the code.
